label: Green steel — AU/Qatar → Korea (cross-border)
description: A cross-border value chain: iron ore + green H2 (Australia), blue H2 (Qatar), and in Korea grid/renewable power, local green H2 (wind→electrolyser), an integrated steel mill, an automaker and a shipbuilder. Imported gas/coal/scrap and capped HBI are buyable. Each facility carries a MACC; the mill can transition iron-making (blast furnace → H2 direct reduction) and steel-making (BOF → EAF). A rising carbon price and declining CO2 cap drive the 2025→2050 transition. Solve jointly (system) or as a value chain (upstream → downstream).

label: Value chain — CCGT power + steel (KR)
description: A value chain (node hierarchy): a Power company owning a CCGT (a GROUP of GT + ST wired by steam) AND a Solar farm (a single component) supplying electricity to a Steel company's integrated mill. Solve jointly, per-company (value chain, upstream→downstream), or pick units.

label: Steel — Korea (POSCO + Hyundai)
description: Two steel companies (POSCO, Hyundai Steel) as a value chain: BF/BOF/EAF facilities wired by intermediate streams, fuel/feedstock blends, transitions to H2-DRI-ESF / EAF under a declining CO2 target and rising carbon price.

label: Aluminium — refinery → smelter → caster
description: A 3-stage value chain (refinery → smelter → caster) wired by intermediate streams; each stage has a lower-carbon transition (H2 refining / RE smelting / electric casting) under a rising carbon price and declining CO2 cap.

label: Shipping — fleet fuel mix
description: Shipping companies and engine-class fleets; fuel blend (bio / LNG / e-fuels) shifts within share bounds under a tightening well-to-wake CO2 target.

label: Petrochemicals — Korea (PLANiT MACC)
description: Process archetypes (Naphtha Cracker / BTX / Utility) as a company hierarchy; naphtha crackers may switch to H2 or electric cracking under a Policy_Target CO2 path.

label: Value chain demo — Korea (group/component)
description: A recursive group hierarchy: value chain → Korea Power / Korea Steel → facilities → machines (the mill runs a blast furnace, BOF and an EAF in parallel). Electricity couples the two companies. Open it in the Chain view to drill the levels; it solves jointly or per-company.
